title: Motor Control Processes Moderate Visual Working Memory Gating Dataset
description: This dataset contains raw EEG recordings and behavioral data from 32 participants performing a visuomotor delayed match-to-sample task with motor and visual interference. The study investigates how motor control processes moderate visual working memory gating using a 2×2 within-subject design manipulating motor interference (same-hand vs. different-hand) and visuomotor interference (motor vs. visuomotor). Participants reported target colors using a knob-controlled color wheel while performing concurrent interference tasks during the memory delay period.
methods_description: EEG recordings were collected during a visuomotor delayed match-to-sample task with 10 blocks of 120 trials each. Stimuli (colored squares or diamonds) were presented for 500 ms followed by a 2900 ms delay. Interference tasks (gray or colored triangles) occurred at 900 ms during the delay in two-thirds of trials, requiring hand-specific responses. Participants reported target color using a knob-controlled color wheel with 4000 ms response window. Inter-trial intervals ranged from 800-1400 ms.
